label: About
title: About The Coral Static Passage
short_blurb: A daily generated front page where the image is the interface.
body: Daily Frontpage is a daily generated interactive front page. Each edition starts from recent saved signals and source research, becomes a new image-led scene, and stays explorable in the archive. The image is the interface: visible marks, objects, and surfaces in the scene open the real sources that shaped the day.

This iteration began with 16 recent saved-signal files and 12 captured links, then narrowed the page to 9 source windows. A source image from f4.bcbits.com helped set the palette and material direction. The selected sources included to be expected, by PINKCOURTESYPHONE and Late '90s / Early '00s Japanese Ambient Techno and Electronica Mix【 THE NTS GUIDE TO… 】, plus 7 more, and the scene became The Coral Static Passage: Borrow the reference image’s washed coral-pink monochrome, faint photographic ghosts, and soft rectangular overlays. Frame a public passage frontally: one dominant form, a huge frosted-glass arch of rose static, floats in waxed plaster with more than half the plate left empty. Two secondary forms hold the space: a narrow inverted curtain panel suspended at left, and a low blackened-steel waterline crossing the floor. Embed quiet anchor details: Rose Static Threshold etched across the arch, Underground Mix on the curtain hem, Bajidoran as a small ochre floor tile, Fabric Acid as a violet pinlight, Air/Earth as twin vent slits, Lucid Landscape as a hairline seam, Broadway as a brass mail slot, Jam as a square moon glyph, Open World as a distant horizon chip. Diffuse rainy light, muted coral, milk white, soot black; hushed after-hours mood. A plate-reading pass mapped 9 visible regions, and the mask audit tightened them against large frosted coral arched threshold, translucent red curtain panel, and ochre floor tile label plate. Review artifacts are in tmp/daily-process-runs/daily-process-2026-04-27T13-59-04-674Z.

typography.rationale: Bricolage Grotesque and Space Grotesk fit record, arcade, and listening-room editions while Fraunces keeps source captions tactile.
